mnemonics.title: Big O vs Omega vs Theta
mnemonics.content: Think: O = 'at most' (Upper), Ω = 'at least' (Lower), Θ = 'exactly equal' (Tight). Remember: O = Outside, Ω = Inside (minimum), Θ = Two-sided equal.

mnemonics.title: Time Complexity Order
mnemonics.content: For asymptotic order remember: 'COnstant LOGarithm Linear Quadratic Exponential Factorial' - C O L Q E F. Or: 'Please Don't Throw Sausage Pizza Every Friday' - P D T S P E F (Power, Doc, Throw, Sausage...).

mnemonics.title: Worst Best Average
mnemonics.content: Worst-case = Maximum (think of O for Upper bound). Best-case = Minimum (think of Ω for Lower bound). Average-case = Expected value.

acronyms.expansion: Time function of input size n
acronyms.usage: Used to express algorithm running time mathematically
When upper and lower bounds are the same

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a graph where f(n) is below c·g(n) for n > n₀ - the function stays below the ceiling (upper bound). Big Omega: f(n) stays above the floor (lower bound). Big Theta: f(n) is sandwiched between floor and ceiling.

Plot graphs: O(1) = horizontal line, O(log n) = slowly rising curve, O(n) = straight diagonal line, O(n²) = parabola, O(2ⁿ) = explodes upward dramatically. The steeper the curve, the worse the complexity.
Count iterations: one loop = n times, two nested = n×n = n², three nested = n³. Draw a triangle where base and height both represent n.

analogies.analogy: Big O is like the worst-case traffic estimate - you'll definitely arrive by that time or sooner.
Big Omega is like the best-case traffic - the minimum time it could take under perfect conditions.
Big Theta is like the exact commute time - it takes approximately that long, not much more or less.
Choosing an algorithm is like choosing a vehicle: O(1) is a helicopter (same time anywhere), O(log n) is an elevator, O(n) is a car, O(n²) is walking backward.
